Inlet Screw Monitoring

Detecting Degradation Before Catastrophic Failure

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

The Application

Inlet screw systems in turbomachinery control fluid flow into compressors and turbines. These precision components operate under high stress and must maintain tight tolerances.

When inlet screws degrade, they cause inefficiency, vibration, and eventual mechanical failure. Detecting this degradation early is critical.

How We Detected It

Samotics' electrical signature analysis monitors the motor driving the inlet screw system. As the screw degrades, it causes:

  • Increased mechanical resistance and torque fluctuations
  • Changes in power consumption patterns
  • Increased harmonic content in the electrical signature
  • Subtle timing changes in the current waveform

Our algorithms detected these changes well before the screw would have failed.

The Benefit

By detecting this fault early, the operator scheduled maintenance and avoided catastrophic failure that would have caused extended downtime and expensive repairs.
